response:
The given parametric equations define a surface known as a **spheroid** or more specifically an **ellipsoid** when $ a \neq b $. The parametric form is similar to spherical coordinates with a scaling factor applied to the $ z $-coordinate. To find the surface area of this shape we need to compute the surface integral of the parametric surface.

### Step 1: Surface Area of the Parametric Surface

Given the parametric equations:

$$
x(u, v) = a \sin(u) \cos(v)
$$
$$
y(u, v) = a \sin(u) \sin(v)
$$
$$
z(u, v) = b \cos(u)
$$

We compute the partial derivatives with respect to $ u $ and $ v $:

$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} = \left( a \cos(u) \cos(v), a \cos(u) \sin(v), -b \sin(u) \right)
$$
$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} = \left( -a \sin(u) \sin(v), a \sin(u) \cos(v), 0 \right)
$$

Next compute the cross product:

$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} = 
\begin{vmatrix}
\mathbf{i} & \mathbf{j} & \mathbf{k} \\
a \cos(u) \cos(v) & a \cos(u) \sin(v) & -b \sin(u) \\
-a \sin(u) \sin(v) & a \sin(u) \cos(v) & 0 \\
\end{vmatrix}
$$

Expanding the determinant:

- \mathbf{j} \left( a \cos(u) \cos(v) \cdot 0 - (-b \sin(u)) \cdot (-a \sin(u) \sin(v)) \right)
